plan_metadata.name: DA Error Message of Legacy Projects

steps.description: Click the English language selector button (flag icon) in the top-right corner of the VS Code welcome screen dialog.
Press Ctrl+Shift+~ in Visual Studio Code to open a new integrated terminal in the workspace view.
Type the text "code --install-extension TeamsDevApp.ms-teams-vscode-extension@6.2.0 --force" into the integrated Terminal (bash) at the bottom panel of Visual Studio Code within the workspace view.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal to execute the command `code --install-extension TeamsDevApp.ms-teams-vscode-extension@6.2.0 --force` within the workspace.
@assertion "successfully installed" exists.
Click the "Close" button located at the top right corner of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface to start creating an agent by declaring instructions and actions.
Click the "No Action" option in the "Add an action to your declarative agent" dropdown within the "Create Declarative Agent" modal in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the "Default folder" selection field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog within Visual Studio Code to choose the project root directory (/home/vscodeAgentToolkitProjects).
Use the keyboard shortcut **Ctrl+Shift+~** in Visual Studio Code to open a new integrated terminal from the Explorer view with the workspace folder  loaded.
Type the command `code --install-extension /app/build-extensions/ms-teams` into the integrated terminal (bash) at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code workspace, within the project.
Press the Tab key in the VS Code terminal to autocomplete the command or file path for "code --install-extension /app/build-extensions/ms-teams".
Type text: '--force' into the integrated terminal of Visual Studio Code (VSCode), within a Bash session, to complete a command for installing a VSIX extension package.
Press the Enter key in the integrated Terminal of Visual Studio Code to execute the command for installing the listed VSIX extension file.
Click the "Explorer" icon in the Visual Studio Code sidebar to open the project file tree.
Click the "Restart Extensions" blue button in the Extensions panel of Visual Studio Code to activate newly installed or updated extensions.

Click the left sidebar "Extensions" icon in Visual Studio Code, opening the Extensions panel for managing and searching extensions. The crosshair at (25, 58) is positioned over the square icon with four squares in the sidebar, visually confirming interaction with the Extensions feature.
Click the "close terminal" button (icon resembling a trash can) in the Terminal panel of Visual Studio Code, closing the active terminal session in the workspace .
Press the "F1" key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code, within the "Get Started with VS Code" walkthrough interface.

Type text: 'Welcome: Open WalkThrough' into the command palette input field of Visual Studio Code. The command palette is accessed using the shortcut "Ctrl+Shift+P" or clicking the "View" menu and selecting "Command Palette," and serves as the main interface for executing commands within the editor.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Welcome: Open Walkthrough" option from the Command Palette within Visual Studio Code.
Type text: 'get started with VS code' into the dropdown field labeled "Select a walkthrough to open" to filter and select the "Get started" walkthrough option in the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the "Enter" key to select the option "Get started with VS Code Built-In" from the dropdown in the search bar at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion 'Get started with VS Code' is visible
Press key: F1 to open the command palette in the Visual Studio Code interface, while on the "Get started with VS Code" walkthrough page.
Type the text 'provision' into the command palette search bar in VS Code, visible at the top-center of the application interface.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" option from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code during the "Setup VS Code" walkthrough, with the editor displaying Copilot feature onboarding.
Type "show notifications" into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top center of the VS Code window.
Press the Enter key to select the highlighted "Notifications: Show Notifications" command from the VS Code command palette.
@assertion notification "actions in provision stage executed successfully" exist.
Press the F1 key in Visual Studio Code to open the command palette, with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension active and the walkthrough guide displayed.
Type the text 'clear all notifications' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field (at the top center of the interface), to trigger the "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" command.
Press the Enter key to select "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" from the VS Code command palette dropdown.
Click the "Share" option under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" panel to change the shared scope of the agent.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of "Share access with selected user(s)" from the dropdown menu in the "Share the agent" dialog within the Visual Studio Code interface.

Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of the sharing scope dropdown option ("Share the agent with selected user(s)") in the "Share the agent with users" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click the input field labeled "Email addresses of users or groups for agent sharing" at the top of the VS Code interface to specify sharing recipients.
Type text: ${{env:MS_AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}} into the input field labeled 'Email addresses of users or groups for agent sharing' within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension panel in Visual Studio Code.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the email address input in the "Email addresses of users or groups for agent sharing" field within the agent sharing interface of Visual Studio Code.
@assertion 'Share feature only supports m365agents.yml version v1.10 or above' is visible
